qa_pairs.question: How do you attach the CephFS network to a virtual machine on SD4H/Juno using the OpenStack command-line client?
qa_pairs.answer: To attach the CephFS network to a virtual machine (VM) on SD4H/Juno using the OpenStack command-line client, first list your servers to identify the VM's ID by running `openstack server list`. Once you have the VM's ID, execute the command `openstack server add network <VM_ID> CephFS-Network`, replacing `<VM_ID>` with the actual ID of your virtual machine.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure for installing the CephFS client packages on an Enterprise Linux 9 virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: To install the CephFS client packages on an Enterprise Linux 9 virtual machine, you need to first configure the relevant repositories. Create the `/etc/yum.repos.d/ceph.repo` file with the specified `[Ceph]`, `[Ceph-noarch]`, and `[ceph-source]` repository configurations, pointing to `http://download.ceph.com/rpm-tentacle/el9/$basearch` and similar paths for noarch and SRPMS, ensuring `gpgcheck=1` and the correct `gpgkey`. Additionally, install the EPEL repository by running `sudo dnf install epel-release`. Finally, install the necessary Ceph client packages with `sudo dnf install -y libcephfs2 python3-cephfs ceph-common python3-ceph-argparse`.

qa_pairs.question: How are the CephFS client and its dependencies installed on a VM running a Debian family distribution?
qa_pairs.answer: To install the CephFS client and its dependencies on a VM running a Debian family distribution, first identify your distribution's codename using `lsb_release -sc`. Then, add the Ceph repository with the command `sudo apt-add-repository 'deb https://download.ceph.com/debian-tentacle/ {codename} main'`, substituting `{codename}` with your distribution's actual codename. If an error occurs, revert the change using `sudo add-apt-repository -r 'deb https://download.ceph.com/debian-tentacle/ {codename} main'`. After setting up the repository, install the Ceph client packages by running `sudo apt-get install -y libcephfs2 python3-cephfs ceph-common python3-ceph-argparse`.
